hypothesis: If a single score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state MD data and its reverse dynamics are guided by isodensity score interpolation between states and refined by Score-Aligned Ascent along r_SAA = s^B − s^A, then it will generate transition-state configurations with near-committor 0.5 and recover multiple reaction pathways more accurately than linear or geodesic interpolation methods, because the interpolated conditional scores approximate the reaction coordinate and SAA concentrates ascent along it while suppressing orthogonal motion.
